title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
During a group discussion, Mr. Marshall proposes that an unknown intruder committed the robbery and murder. Mickey challenges this theory, suggesting that the group is being dishonest about their true beliefs. Mary Darland supports Marshall's stance, while Philip Darland warns Mickey that speaking too much in their current "tight spot" is dangerous. Kirsten Lindström also rebukes Mickey, telling him to mind his own business and stop acting like a reckless child. Following this tense exchange, the seven individuals retire to their rooms at Sunny Corner, though none are able to sleep soundly.

Philip Darwent, an intelligent man whose physical illness has made him highly observant of human nature, reflects on his marriage to Mary. He acknowledges that while he loves her and values her wealth, he feels a need to escape her intense devotion. He contemplates the death of his mother-in-law, Mrs. Aggles, noting that while he did not like her, her murder is a repulsive event. He suspects the perpetrator may be one of her "adopted" sons.

As the group prepares for the arrival of Detective Inspector Rush, Philip discusses the upcoming interrogation with Mary. He warns her that the detective will question her regarding her activities on November 9th. Philip advises Mary to be careful with her alibi, noting that although she previously claimed to be in their room with him, she had actually left the room between 7:00 and 7:30.
